sutta: and 50 9 once while the exalted 1 was dwelling near kosambi in gosita park the venerable udain surrounded by a great gathering of laymen said teaching dhamma now the venerable ananda saw the venerable udain so seated teaching and there it went to the exalted 1 saluted and sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 lord the venerable udyin surrounded by a great gathering teaches dhamma and the exalted 1 said verily ananda not easy is it to teach dhamma to others in teaching others dhamma ananda make 5 things stand up within you then teach others dhamma what 5 teach others dhamma thinking i will talk the talk on the gradual teach others dhamma thinking i will talk a talk with the way with the way or practice in view teach others dhamma thinking i will talk a talk out of kindliness teach teach others dhamma thinking i will talk or talk not as a means for gain teach others dhamma thinking i will talk a ton not to my own hurt nor to others verily ananda not easy is it to teach dhamma to others in teaching others dhamma make these 5 things stand up within you then teach others dhamma that's the end of the sutta
commentary: the other day we heard 1 su tower the buddha said that he has grey reverence for the dhamma even the dhamma raja the dhamma king respects the dhamma as his rajana so that is why in teaching dhamma we have to be uh to take pains to teach them and the buddha said these 5 things 1 is to talk a gradual teaching the second 1 talk with the practice of the way in view that means beneficial topic top out of kindliness the fourth 1 talk not as a means for gain not with some ulterior motive and the last 1 talk not to my own hurt nor to others that means you don't get personal don't [Music] criticize others on a personal level but we can criticize dhamma in fact in the sukta earlier we heard the buddha said what what we know is not dhamma we should point out that it is not dhamma and what is dharma we should point out as the correct number the next 2 times

items: make 5 things stand up within you
talk with the practice of the way in view
talk not as a means for gain
